mechanism_narrative: Steady month-over-month growth in nonfarm payrolls signals expanding aggregate income and consumption, which mechanically lifts corporate revenues and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns following positive PAYEMS surprises are negative in more than 50% of out-of-sample months.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate is pinned below 0.25%, sustained zero-interest-rate policy suppresses Treasury yields and forces capital reallocation into equities, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:Fed funds rate held near zero since December 2008
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces negative 5-day returns in more than 55% of weeks when FEDFUNDS is below 0.25%.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous acceleration in industrial production and declining unemployment creates a virtuous cycle of output and labor income growth that amplifies earnings expectations, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:IP expanding while unemployment falls through 2014
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05) during months when INDPRO increases and UNRATE decreases concurrently.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts growth drives positive S&P 500 returns over 60 days specifically during the post-crisis recovery phase because construction multipliers are largest when inventory is depleted.
evidence_refs: fact:Housing starts remained below historical averages in 2014
regime_hint: post-crisis recovery with depressed housing inventory
failure_condition: HOUST month-over-month changes show a rank correlation below 0.03 with 60-day forward returns during 2012-2014.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals a tightening labor market that boosts wage growth expectations and consumer spending, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:Unemployment fell from 10% in 2009 to 8.1% by September 2014
failure_condition: S&P 500 5-day returns are negative following monthly UNRATE drops greater than 0.2pp in more than 50% of instances.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production without a corresponding increase in retail sales indicates an inventory accumulation that precedes production cutbacks and margin pressure, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 60-day returns are positive in more than 55% of periods when INDPRO rises while RSAFS stagnates.

mechanism_narrative: Strong quarterly real GDP growth reflects broad-based final demand expansion that validates consensus earnings forecasts and reduces recession risk premia, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Quarterly GDPC1 growth surprises show rank IC below 0.02 for 20-day forward returns over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: CPI acceleration above a stable threshold forces the market to discount earlier Federal Reserve tightening, compressing equity multiples and generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 5-day returns are positive following triggered CPIAUCSL acceleration in more than 50% of instances.
